title: The Event Loop: Single-Threaded Concurrency
overview: A programming construct that waits for and dispatches events or messages, enabling non-blocking execution in a single-threaded environment.
historical_context: Gained massive popularity with the Web Explosion (JavaScript) as a way to handle high-concurrency I/O without the overhead of threads.
mental_model: Think of a restaurant waiter who takes orders and moves on to the next table while the kitchen is cooking, instead of standing still.
tradeoffs: CPU-bound tasks can "block" the loop, freezing the entire application.
legacy: The heartbeat of the modern web (Node.js, Browser JS) and Python’s asyncio.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Compare the Node.js event loop with Python’s asyncio event loop.
Explain how the "Callback Queue" interacts with the "Call Stack".
